jazztraditionalpop.label: Jazz & Traditional Pop
jazztraditionalpop.description: Rooted in early 20th-century songcraft and performance, this taxonomy covers the smooth vocals, swing rhythms, and orchestral arrangements that shaped pre-rock popular music.

countryfolk.label: Country & Folk
countryfolk.description: Drawing from rural storytelling and acoustic traditions, Country & Folk blends heartfelt lyricism and simple melodies, ranging from old-time ballads to polished country pop.

rock.label: Rock
rock.description: Evolving from 1950s rhythm and blues, Rock centers on electric guitars, drums, and a strong backbeat, encompassing everything from early rock & roll to modern alternative sounds.

soulrnb.label: Soul & R&B
soulrnb.description: Built on rhythm, groove, and emotional expression, Soul & R&B highlight smooth vocals, gospel influence, and the evolution to contemporary production styles.

dance.label: Dance & Electronic
dance.description: Driven by rhythm and technology, Dance & Electronic music spans disco to modern EDM, defined by synthesizers, drum machines, and production designed for movement.

hiphop.label: Hip-hop
hiphop.description: Hip Hop fuses rhythmic beats and spoken lyrics, evolving from early disco rap into diverse global forms like trap and pop rap.

2-Step.label: 2-Step
2-Step.description: Originating in London and popular in the late 1990s; jittery, shuffling rhythms rarely following a four-on-the-floor pattern.

A cappella.label: A cappella
A cappella.description: Any kind of vocal performance without instrumental accompaniment.

Abstract Hip Hop.label: Abstract Hip Hop
Abstract Hip Hop.description: Uses much more abstract lyricism than what is considered conventional within the genre, typically through metaphors, symbolism, and cryptic meanings.

Acid Breaks.label: Acid Breaks
Acid Breaks.description: Incorporates prominent 'squelching' resonant low pass filter sounds typical to Acid House into syncopated, broken rhythms between 110–150 BPM.

Acid House.label: Acid House
Acid House.description: Emerged in the mid-1980s, characterised by repetitive basslines created with the distinctive modulated 'squelching' sound of the Roland TB-303 synthesiser.

Acoustic Rock.label: Acoustic Rock
Acoustic Rock.description: Rock with acoustic instrumentation, as opposed to the typical electric instrumentation.

Adult Contemporary.label: Adult Contemporary
Adult Contemporary.description: Slow to midtempo with a light and soothing tone, emerged as a successful radio format in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

Afrobeats.label: Afrobeats
Afrobeats.description: Originated in West Africa during the early 2010s and includes elements from Pop Rap, Contemporary R&B, and Dancehall.

Alt-Pop.label: Alt-Pop
Alt-Pop.description: Emerged in the late 2000s and early 2010s, combines chart Pop conventions with alternative/indie genre sensibilities, sometimes on a more minimal and contemplative atmosphere, often extracted from Alternative R&B.

Alternative Dance.label: Alternative Dance
Alternative Dance.description: Incorporates rhythms and synthesizers from Electronic Dance Music within the context of Alternative Rock and related styles; predominantly popular in the 1990s.

Alternative Metal.label: Alternative Metal
Alternative Metal.description: Mid-tempo style emphasizing melodicism and rhythmic heaviness; branches outside of the stylistic conventions of Metal.

Alternative R&B.label: Alternative R&B
Alternative R&B.description: Deviating from the traditional styles of Contemporary R&B and featuring smooth, reverberated, Electronic-influenced production with heavy bass and often reverberated vocals.

Alternative Rock.label: Alternative Rock
Alternative Rock.description: Performed with a less commercial sensibility, utilizing more eccentric, Punk-influenced sounds, moodier or quirkier lyricism, and sometimes ample amounts of distortion, often paired with Pop-influenced songwriting.

American Folk.label: American Folk
American Folk.description: Traces back through the history of European and West African settlement and migration to North America, including parts of northern Mexico and Canada in addition to the United States.

Americana.label: Americana
Americana.description: Incorporates a broad swath of American Folk Music and Contemporary Folk influences, evolving alongside Alt-Country.

AOR.label: AOR
AOR.description: Adult Oriented Rock. Emerged in the late 1970s and early 1980s as an amalgamation of Hard Rock, Pop Rock, and Progressive Rock; characterized by a rich, layered sound, slick production and a heavy reliance on commercial melodic hooks.

Appalachian Folk Music.label: Appalachian Folk Music
Appalachian Folk Music.description: Folk of the Appalachian Mountains in the Eastern United States.

Art Pop.label: Art Pop
Art Pop.description: Inventive, idiosyncratic, or artistically ambitious, often experimenting with unconventional musical elements and conceptual approaches while retaining accessibility.

Art Rock.label: Art Rock
Art Rock.description: Attempts to expand the bounds of Rock within conventional structures, often using outside influences or experimentation for conceptual or thematic purpose.

Atlanta Bass.label: Atlanta Bass
Atlanta Bass.description: Originated in Atlanta, slower tempos and a more melodic approach than Miami Bass.

Australian Folk Music.label: Australian Folk Music
Australian Folk Music.description: Folk traditions of the European colonists and convicts in Australia.

Baggy.label: Baggy
Baggy.description: Blends Dance and Funk-based grooves with a laid-back, psychedelic atmosphere; frequently features jangly guitars, looping basslines, four-bar drum patterns, and a hypnotic, carefree feel that originated in the Madchester scene of the late 1980s.

Bakersfield Sound.label: Bakersfield Sound
Bakersfield Sound.description: A genre of Country music from the 1950s, named after its origin in Bakersfield, California. It is based heavily on electric instrumentation and a defined backbeat, like incorporating Rock & Roll into country.

Balearic Beat.label: Balearic Beat
Balearic Beat.description: Variety of relaxed, laid-back music that originates from the Balearic Islands off the coast of Spain, and particularly the club scene in Ibiza.

Ballroom.label: Ballroom
Ballroom.description: Emerging from the predominantly Black and LGBT Harlem ballroom culture, characterized by hard-hitting rhythms, generally improvised chanting by MCs known as commentators, and a distinctive crash sample.

Baltimore Club.label: Baltimore Club
Baltimore Club.description: Drew on the 'booty bass' syncopation of Miami Bass, combined with Breakbeat rhythms around 130 BPM and chopped up vocal samples as found in Ghetto House.

Barbershop.label: Barbershop
Barbershop.description: Harmonized singing consisting of four vocal elements: the 'lead' (2nd tenor), the 1st tenor, the baritone, and the bass.

Baroque Pop.label: Baroque Pop
Baroque Pop.description: Incorporates elements of Western Classical Music, commonly featuring harpsichord or strings as well as the rhythmic flow and counterpoint of Baroque Music.

Beat.label: Beat
Beat.description: Emerged in the early 1960s in the United Kingdom, drawing on Rock & Roll and the late 1950s Skiffle revival in a simple guitar-band format with strong melodies.

Beatboxing.label: Beatboxing
Beatboxing.description: Form of vocal percussion developed during the 1980s, primarily involving the art of producing drum beats, rhythm, and musical sounds using one's mouth, lips, tongue, voice, and more.

Bedroom Pop.label: Bedroom Pop
Bedroom Pop.description: Named for solo producers and singer-songwriters from the 2010s onward who created intimate, 'authentic' music in their own homes, outside of a traditional studio environment.

Big Band.label: Big Band
Big Band.description: Commonly associated with the golden era of Swing in the 1930s and 1940s, performed by a large ensemble including brass, woodwinds, and usually a rhythm section.

Big Beat.label: Big Beat
Big Beat.description: Popular in Britain during the late 1990s, driven by edited breakbeats and prominent basslines.

Big Music.label: Big Music
Big Music.description: Rooted in Post-Punk and popular in the 1980s, with anthemic songwriting and a grandiose, stadium-ready sound.

Big Room House.label: Big Room House
Big Room House.description: Energetic, festival-oriented style characterized by heavy reverb, intense build-ups, strong kicks, minimalistic drop melodies, and sparse, raw breakdowns.

Bitpop.label: Bitpop
Bitpop.description: Fuses Bit Music with additional synths, beats, guitars and modern production values, emphasizing highly catchy melodies and relatively fast tempos.

Blue-Eyed Soul.label: Blue-Eyed Soul
Blue-Eyed Soul.description: Usually refers to a Pop or Pop Rock song that contains strong Gospel-like elements, primarily popularized by white American and British singers in the 1960s through the 1980s.

Blues Rock.label: Blues Rock
Blues Rock.description: Rock that relies on the chords/scales and instrumental improvisation of Blues.

Bolero.label: Bolero
Bolero.description: Ballad with a 2/4 time signature that appeared in eastern Cuba towards the end of the 19th century.

Boogie.label: Boogie
Boogie.description: Continuation of the Disco sound with increased influence from Funk, Synth Funk, and R&B and a slower tempo, often featuring drum machines and filter modulated synthesizers.

Boogie Rock.label: Boogie Rock
Boogie Rock.description: Straightforward groove-oriented style influenced by Rock & Roll and Boogie Woogie.

Boogie Woogie.label: Boogie Woogie
Boogie Woogie.description: Early, uptempo Blues featuring the piano as the main instrument, with soloing and improvisation played over the top of a repetitive eight note bassline.

Boom Bap.label: Boom Bap
Boom Bap.description: Production style identified by an acoustic-sounding drum pattern of a kick drum on the downbeat followed by a cracking snare on the upbeat.

Bounce.label: Bounce
Bounce.description: Developed in New Orleans in the early 1990s, initially defined by a slow to midtempo beat with a double-time hi-hat on top.

Boy Band.label: Boy Band
Boy Band.description: Male vocal groups often put together by talent managers or record producers.

Breakbeat.label: Breakbeat
Breakbeat.description: Umbrella of affiliated Electronic Dance Music styles broadly featuring prominent syncopated drum patterns (called breakbeats) that emerged from Breakbeat Hardcore's initial influence, not including styles directly related to Drum and Bass and Jungle.

Breakstep.label: Breakstep
Breakstep.description: Hard-hitting Breakbeat rhythms and heavy, rolling basslines.

Brill Building.label: Brill Building
Brill Building.description: Elaborate, polished, and typically melodramatic composition and arrangement associated with the songwriting teams at the Brill office building during the late 1950s and early 1960s.

British Dance Band.label: British Dance Band
British Dance Band.description: Came from the dancehalls of the 1920s and 1930s, combining a British take on Jazz with the Music Hall tradition.

British Rhythm & Blues.label: British Rhythm & Blues
British Rhythm & Blues.description: A style infusing Rock and R&B that was hugely popular in the United Kingdom between 1963 and 1966.

Bro-Country.label: Bro-Country
Bro-Country.description: 21st century style with an often party-oriented sound and lyrical content, taking strong influence from Hard Rock, Hip Hop and Pop.

Bubblegum.label: Bubblegum
Bubblegum.description: Upbeat, catchy, simple, and targeted at children and young teens; especially popular in the 1960s and 1970s.

Bubblegum Dance.label: Bubblegum Dance
Bubblegum Dance.description: Playful, childlike style with a Bubblegum-like atmosphere that often incorporates high-pitched, light-hearted, and usually female-fronted vocals.

Cabaret.label: Cabaret
Cabaret.description: Entertainment featuring Comedy, song, dance, and theatre.

Calypso.label: Calypso
Calypso.description: Genre from Trinidad and Tobago featuring a brass section and percussion.

Canzone Napoletana.label: Canzone Napoletana
Canzone Napoletana.description: Sung in the Neapolitan language by a male voice with instrumental accompaniment, originating in the Italian city of Naples and the surrounding regions.

Celtic Folk Music.label: Celtic Folk Music
Celtic Folk Music.description: Derived from Celtic traditions of Ireland, Scotland, Wales, Cornwall, the Isle of Man, Brittany, Galicia, Cantabria, Asturias, and Trás-os-Montes (Portugal).

Chamber Pop.label: Chamber Pop
Chamber Pop.description: Based on the smooth and melodic songwriting of Lounge and other Easy Listening, as well as the orchestration of Baroque Pop and the production styles of other 1960s pop genres.

Chicago House.label: Chicago House
Chicago House.description: Umbrella term for a broad variety of House that originated from Chicago in the (mostly mid-to-late) 1980s and early 90s.

Children's Music.label: Children's Music
Children's Music.description: Music recorded for an intended audience of children.

Chillwave.label: Chillwave
Chillwave.description: Emulates vintage aesthetics and popular musical styles of the 1980s/1990s altered by modern recording technology to create a hazy dreamlike atmosphere.

Chipmunk Soul.label: Chipmunk Soul
Chipmunk Soul.description: Sped-up vocal samples and slick, soulful production.

Christian Rock.label: Christian Rock
Christian Rock.description: Incorporates Christian lyrics, with topics such as faith, metaphysics, or biblical characters.

Classical Crossover.label: Classical Crossover
Classical Crossover.description: Takes specific Western Classical Music conventions and applies them into a straightforward Pop context for mainstream appeal.

Close Harmony.label: Close Harmony
Close Harmony.description: Traditional two-part harmony singing performed mostly by brothers.

Cloud Rap.label: Cloud Rap
Cloud Rap.description: Emerged in the early 2010s, known for its relaxed, dream-like, and reverb-heavy production style, taking influence from Chillwave, and producers' obscure sample choices.

Cocktail Nation.label: Cocktail Nation
Cocktail Nation.description: Decidedly ironic take on Lounge that emerged in the early 1990s.

Comedy Rap.label: Comedy Rap
Comedy Rap.description: Primarily intended to be humorous, through the use of comedic lyrics, production, delivery, or other musical aspects.

Comedy Rock.label: Comedy Rock
Comedy Rock.description: Roughly defined as a mix of Rock or any of its subgenres with elements of Comedy such as Satire and Musical Parody, as well as concepts of Novelty.

Conjunto Andino.label: Conjunto Andino
Conjunto Andino.description: Urban folk ensembles using musical instruments of Andean Indigenous origin or association; despite frequent association with Indigenous villages, they bear little resemblance to the Indigenous expressions of the Southern Andes.

Conscious Hip Hop.label: Conscious Hip Hop
Conscious Hip Hop.description: Artists whose lyrics deal with social issues.

Contemporary Country.label: Contemporary Country
Contemporary Country.description: Incorporates both heavily Pop-influenced styles, and the more pop-averse movement of Neo-Traditionalist Country.

Contemporary Folk.label: Contemporary Folk
Contemporary Folk.description: Generally acoustic and rooted in modernized Traditional Folk Music, originated with the 20th century American folk revival.

Contemporary R&B.label: Contemporary R&B
Contemporary R&B.description: Soulful, passionate and melismatic vocals originally derived from popular Soul and Disco, and by smooth, lush and often synthesized production variously influenced by Hip Hop, Funk, and related strands of Pop.

Country Blues.label: Country Blues
Country Blues.description: Various styles of Blues originating in the rural US South, typically relying on expressive vocal delivery and featuring simple, usually acoustic instrumentation.

Country Boogie.label: Country Boogie
Country Boogie.description: Uptempo style built around the Boogie Woogie rhythm, with strong beats and prominent electric guitar leads, made popular in the 1940s.

Country Gospel.label: Country Gospel
Country Gospel.description: Traditional Country style that heavily incorporates Gospel music.

Country Pop.label: Country Pop
Country Pop.description: Combination of Country with Pop to appeal to a larger audience.

Country Rap.label: Country Rap
Country Rap.description: Combines the flow, production-emphasis, and demeanor of Hip Hop with the lyrical content and general approach of Country.

Country Rock.label: Country Rock
Country Rock.description: Combination of contemporary Rock with more traditional American Country.

Country Soul.label: Country Soul
Country Soul.description: Combining the gritty horn-driven sound of Southern Soul with the textures and aesthetics of Country.

Countrypolitan.label: Countrypolitan
Countrypolitan.description: Emphasizes Pop-oriented orchestration and songwriting while reducing the role of Country instrumentation and rhythms; often featuring lush string arrangements and group backing vocals.

Cowpunk.label: Cowpunk
Cowpunk.description: Mixes together Country Rock, Rockabilly, and Contemporary Folk with the familiar punk sound.

Crunk.label: Crunk
Crunk.description: Originating from the southern United States and reliant on drum machines and synthesizer stabs to create an energetic, often dance-oriented sound.

Cumbia.label: Cumbia
Cumbia.description: Danceable folk genre formed during the 19th century in Colombia and eastern Panama.

Cumbia Pop.label: Cumbia Pop
Cumbia Pop.description: Developed in the Río de la Plata region in the mid-2010s, featuring Latin Pop influences and lyrics related to romance, youth, and fun.

Cumbiatón.label: Cumbiatón
Cumbiatón.description: Combining Reggaetón with Cumbia mexicana, and less frequently with other forms of traditional Mexican Music.

Dance-Pop.label: Dance-Pop
Dance-Pop.description: Simple, yet catchy melodies and throbbing beats inspired initially by Disco and later various House styles.

Dance-Punk.label: Dance-Punk
Dance-Punk.description: Mixes the energy of Punk Rock with the danceable rhythms of Funk and Disco.

Dance-Punk Revival.label: Dance-Punk Revival
Dance-Punk Revival.description: Punchy and danceable variant of Post-Punk Revival that features Electronic instrumentation and influence from Alternative Dance.

Dancefloor Drum and Bass.label: Dancefloor Drum and Bass
Dancefloor Drum and Bass.description: Emerged in the late 2000s as a more accessible and polished style with melodic, prominent synth leads derived from Jump-Up and punchy, often synthesized drums.

Dancehall.label: Dancehall
Dancehall.description: Developed from Reggae in the late 1970s, when Roots Reggae became more stripped-down, with an increased emphasis on pure rhythm.

Dark Cabaret.label: Dark Cabaret
Dark Cabaret.description: Dark, moody style incorporating elements of Cabaret in a modern context, with varied instrumentation.

Deep House.label: Deep House
Deep House.description: Emerged in the United States during the mid-1980s, featuring a relaxed tempo and influences from Jazz, Funk, and Soul.

Deep Soul.label: Deep Soul
Deep Soul.description: Draws on the typical Southern Soul groove and accentuates the Gospel and Blues influence, particularly Chicago Blues.

Digital Dancehall.label: Digital Dancehall
Digital Dancehall.description: First, pre-Ragga style of Dancehall to popularize Electronic rhythms that arose in the mid-1980s.

Digital Hardcore.label: Digital Hardcore
Digital Hardcore.description: High-tempo fusion of Hardcore Punk and Electronic, combining harsh vocals and electric guitars of the former with electronic instruments.

Dirty South.label: Dirty South
Dirty South.description: Originated in the Southern US during the mid-1990s, defined by a bouncy rhythm, fast-tempo snares, 808s, heavy bass, and often rapped in a rhythmic delivery consisting of explicit lyrics.

Disco.label: Disco
Disco.description: Combines the lush orchestration of Philly Soul and bass grooves of Funk with a four-on-the-floor rhythm.

Disco Rap.label: Disco Rap
Disco Rap.description: Most prevalent from 1979 to 1982, featuring MCs rapping over popular Disco breaks.

Diva House.label: Diva House
Diva House.description: Dominant, anthemic female vocals, common use of piano stabs, and energetic, driving four-on-the-floor rhythms.

Doo-Wop.label: Doo-Wop
Doo-Wop.description: Heavy emphasis on male four-part harmonies and light instrumentation; peaked in popularity in the 1950s.

Downtempo.label: Downtempo
Downtempo.description: Atmospheric and groove-based with relaxed tempos and mellow beats.

Dream Pop.label: Dream Pop
Dream Pop.description: Reverberated guitars, effects-laden vocals, and dense productions, creating a psychedelic, spacious, and dreamlike sound.

Drone.label: Drone
Drone.description: Focuses on long, sustained tones and tone clusters, or repetition of single notes.

Dutch House.label: Dutch House
Dutch House.description: High-pitched wonky synth melodies and heavy influence from Fidget House which developed in the Netherlands around 2007–08.

Easy Listening.label: Easy Listening
Easy Listening.description: Typically instrumental, including lush arrangements and melodies played by orchestras or big bands aiming for light entertainment.

Electro Hop.label: Electro Hop
Electro Hop.description: Emerged in the mid-2000s featuring upbeat rap verses and catchy hooks over four-on-the-floor Electropop and Electro House-inspired production.

Electro House.label: Electro House
Electro House.description: Warm analog basslines, punchy percussion, crisp high-pitched synthesizer leads, and clipped vocal samples.

Electro Swing.label: Electro Swing
Electro Swing.description: Upbeat, energetic style that splices Swing with Electronic Dance Music, looking to recapture the atmosphere of late 1920s to mid-1940s Jazz within a more updated club-friendly medium.

Electro-Disco.label: Electro-Disco
Electro-Disco.description: Disco instrumentation is largely replaced by synthesizers and augmented with futuristic elements such as the vocoder.

Electroclash.label: Electroclash
Electroclash.description: Draws from 1980s styles such as Electro, New Wave, and Electro-Disco combined with Techno, resulting in minimal, throbbing beats and basslines, buzzing synthesizer parts, vocoders and often monotone, half-spoken, half-sung vocals.

Electropop.label: Electropop
Electropop.description: Dense, layered, and compressed production, usually coupled with a distinct fuzzy and warm low-frequency synthesizer style.

Emo Rap.label: Emo Rap
Emo Rap.description: Moody, melancholic production combining atmospheric Cloud Rap, and Trap's typical drum programming, with sampling from Alternative Rock, Midwest Emo, Emo-Pop, and Pop Punk.

Euro House.label: Euro House
Euro House.description: Vocal-focused style emerging in the late 1980s in Europe, developing in parallel with Eurodance.

Euro Trance.label: Euro Trance
Euro Trance.description: Emerged as a fusion with Eurodance, featuring fast tempos, more accessible grooves and structures, and simple, catchy melodies with vocals and saw synth leads.

Euro-Disco.label: Euro-Disco
Euro-Disco.description: European style that moved away from the genre's Rhythm & Blues and Soul roots, speeding up the tempo and adding Electronic instruments like synthesizers and drum machines as a backing for more melodic Europop-influenced vocals.

Eurobeat.label: Eurobeat
Eurobeat.description: Fast-paced with roots in Hi-NRG and Italo-Disco; became mainly popular in Japan in the 1990s.

Eurodance.label: Eurodance
Eurodance.description: Takes elements of Euro-Disco, House, Techno, and Hi-NRG while featuring on-beat kick, hi-hat and snare sounds at around 110 to 150 bpm.

Europop.label: Europop
Europop.description: Emerged throughout mainland Europe in the early 1970s, referring to slick, highly commercial Pop songs that emphasize sugary melodies and light, bouncy instrumentation.

Exotica.label: Exotica
Exotica.description: Emerged in the early 1950s as the United States developed a fascination with Polynesia and the South Pacific, incorporates traditional instruments and rhythms from these cultures into a Western Pop context.

Experimental Hip Hop.label: Experimental Hip Hop
Experimental Hip Hop.description: Incorporates experimental and eccentric elements considered unconventional or unorthodox compared to more traditional hip hop.

Fanfare.label: Fanfare
Fanfare.description: Brass-led tradition originating in Western Europe, commonly performed during important public events, typically featuring a ceremonious, triumphant, or uplifting sound.

Festival Progressive House.label: Festival Progressive House
Festival Progressive House.description: Uplifting, melodic, energetic but clean drops, featuring modern supersaws and pianos, taking influences from Progressive House and Uplifting Trance, but applying them to a structure closer to Big Room House and Electro House.

Festival Trap.label: Festival Trap
Festival Trap.description: Massive, sub-bass-heavy drops with simple melodies, synth leads, and production influenced by Big Room House; highly popular from 2013 to 2015.

Fidget House.label: Fidget House
Fidget House.description: Makes heavy use of Breakbeat Hardcore-esque synth stabs, pitch bending of basslines, and cut-up vocal samples, as well as glitchy 4/4 rhythms.

Film Soundtrack.label: Film Soundtrack
Film Soundtrack.description: Music from a movie, including vocal songs and/or instrumental film scores.

Flamenco.label: Flamenco
Flamenco.description: Traditional Spanish style based around fiery acoustic guitar rhythms, emotional vocals, and accompaniment from traditional percussion and dance.

Flamenco Pop.label: Flamenco Pop
Flamenco Pop.description: Incorporates Flamenco sub-styles into a more commercial Pop form; particularly through the vocal style and rhythms.

Florida Breaks.label: Florida Breaks
Florida Breaks.description: Developed during the mid-1990s, combining major influences of Hip Hop derivatives such as Miami Bass and Electro with elements of 1990s Electronic Dance genres like House or Techno.

Folk Pop.label: Folk Pop
Folk Pop.description: Blends Contemporary Folk with a Pop sensibility, most popular in the late 1960s and early 1970s, featuring influence from the contemporary Folk Rock style.

Folk Rock.label: Folk Rock
Folk Rock.description: Influenced by Folk, particularly in the use of acoustic instrumentation and simple arrangements; has its origins in the American Contemporary Folk movement of the 1960s.

Folkhop.label: Folkhop
Folkhop.description: Influenced by Hip Hop, including looped beats with Bhangra instrumentation and rapped vocals.

Football Chant.label: Football Chant
Football Chant.description: Sung by large crowds at football (soccer) games to celebrate or insult teams.

Freakbeat.label: Freakbeat
Freakbeat.description: Outgrowth of the Mod movement combining British Rhythm & Blues and Beat with studio effects common in Psychedelia; often seen as a British relative of the American Garage Psych scene.

Freestyle.label: Freestyle
Freestyle.description: Electro-influenced dance style developed in Latino communities in New York and Miami in the 1980s, dominated by Latin Freestyle and its Latin influences.

French Electro.label: French Electro
French Electro.description: Mixes Electro House with elements of Electroclash, Nu-Disco, Synth Funk, and Electro-Industrial, resulting in a harsh, punchy, and funky midtempo sound.

French House.label: French House
French House.description: Originated in France in the mid-1990s, characterized by the use of filtered samples of Funk and Disco.

Funk.label: Funk
Funk.description: Danceable, syncopated grooves carried by bass and drums alongside percussive guitar, keyboard, and horn parts, emphasizing the downbeat.

Funk Metal.label: Funk Metal
Funk Metal.description: Melds Funk Rock grooves with slap basslines and metallic guitarwork.

Funk Rock.label: Funk Rock
Funk Rock.description: Fusion of Rock and Funk that emerged in the early 1970s, emphasizing funk grooves over a rock foundation.

Funktronica.label: Funktronica
Funktronica.description: Originated in eastern North America around the early 2000s, combining elements of Synth Funk and Italo-Disco.

Funky Breaks.label: Funky Breaks
Funky Breaks.description: Merges popular 1990s breakbeat styles like Big Beat with Funk and Soul elements.

Funky House.label: Funky House
Funky House.description: Funky, warm, catchy basslines over looped Funk or Disco samples, resulting in an upbeat, bouncy groove.

Future Bass.label: Future Bass
Future Bass.description: Woozy, detuned synthesizers, syncopated percussion, and melodic, carefree atmospheres.

Future House.label: Future House
Future House.description: Features metallic-sounding drops and frequency-modulated basslines at around 120–130 BPM.

Futuristic Swag.label: Futuristic Swag
Futuristic Swag.description: Playful, Snap-influenced and party-oriented Hip Hop style.

G-Funk.label: G-Funk
G-Funk.description: Relaxed and organic sound featuring live instrumentation, use of P-Funk samples, and high-pitched synth lines.

Garage House.label: Garage House
Garage House.description: Disco-influenced style of House that developed in the 1980s, incorporating elements of Gospel, Soul, and Dub, before evolving into a more upbeat style that prominently features crisp syncopated hi-hats and synth stabs.

Garage Rock.label: Garage Rock
Garage Rock.description: Raw and energetic, generally employing simple, sloppy, and fuzzbox-distorted guitar melodies in addition to frequent shouting or screaming.

Geek Rock.label: Geek Rock
Geek Rock.description: Emphasizes a nerdy aesthetic, incorporating synthesizers and other instrumentation less common in Alternative Rock alongside quirky Pop-oriented songwriting and dense, witty lyricism on unconventional topics.

Glam Metal.label: Glam Metal
Glam Metal.description: Very catchy hooks and guitar riffs, sing-along choruses, and lyrics often glamorizing the 'sex, drugs and rock & roll' lifestyle.

Glam Punk.label: Glam Punk
Glam Punk.description: Contrasts a snotty, nihilistic, and combative attitude of punk with theatrics and flamboyance.

Glam Rock.label: Glam Rock
Glam Rock.description: Incorporates elements of Rock & Roll and Blues Rock into catchy Pop Rock melodies, usually accompanied with a strong sense of theatricality.

Glitch Pop.label: Glitch Pop
Glitch Pop.description: Incorporates elements of more experimental Electronic styles like IDM and Glitch.

Go-Go.label: Go-Go
Go-Go.description: Dominated by drums and percussion (primarily congas) with heavily syncopated rhythms, bursts of horns, and call-and-response vocals.

Gospel.label: Gospel
Gospel.description: Arising from vocal Christian tradition often featuring emotive performances, layered accompaniments, and themes of faith and salvation.

Gothic Metal.label: Gothic Metal
Gothic Metal.description: Features heavy, low-mid tempo riffs, somber, gloomy atmosphere accentuated by ethereal keyboards and synthesizer layers, and vocal styles ranging from raspy growls to female operatic singing.

Grebo.label: Grebo
Grebo.description: Short-lived English subculture and associated music; raw, futuristic, sample-heavy form of Alternative Dance with a dirty guitar sound.

Grunge.label: Grunge
Grunge.description: Mixes Punk and Metal sensibilities into a mid-tempo approach featuring a heavy, 'sludgy' guitar sound, 'raspy' vocals and angsty lyricism.

Halloween Music.label: Halloween Music
Halloween Music.description: Associated with the secular holiday of Halloween.

Hard Rock.label: Hard Rock
Hard Rock.description: Originated in the mid-to-late 1960s from Psychedelic Rock and Blues Rock with a heavy sound characterized by distorted guitars and power chords.

Hardcore Hip Hop.label: Hardcore Hip Hop
Hardcore Hip Hop.description: Confrontation and aggression, whether in the lyricism, vocal delivery, hard beats, heavy production, or any combination thereof.

Heartland Rock.label: Heartland Rock
Heartland Rock.description: Borrows elements from Folk Rock, Roots Rock, and 1950s–60s Rock & Roll; often characterized by its conscious, communal lyrical concerns and working-class appeal.

Heavy Metal.label: Heavy Metal
Heavy Metal.description: Heavier outgrowth of Hard Rock featuring greater distortion and intensity along with lesser Blues influences.

Heavy Psych.label: Heavy Psych
Heavy Psych.description: Developed in the late 1960s with influence from Blues Rock, such as heavy dependence on riffs and willingness to improvise, adding crushing levels of heaviness with thick fuzzy guitars to create a doomy and morose atmosphere.

Hi-NRG.label: Hi-NRG
Hi-NRG.description: Emerged in the late 1970s and quickly became the most popular form of Electronic Dance Music in the LGBT club scenes of San Francisco and New York, characterized by its uptempo, high-energy sound.

Hip Hop.label: Hip Hop
Hip Hop.description: Emerged primarily on the United States east coast in African American communities in the late 1970s; emphasizes rhythmic beat patterns and a type of spoken vocal delivery known as rapping.

Hip Hop Soul.label: Hip Hop Soul
Hip Hop Soul.description: Hip Hop-oriented style that evolved out of New Jack Swing in the early 1990s.

Hip House.label: Hip House
Hip House.description: Incorporates production elements of early Hip Hop and usually rapping into 1980s House.

Honky Tonk.label: Honky Tonk
Honky Tonk.description: Upbeat style that retains much of the common instrumentation of Traditional Country, as well as featuring prominent guitars (especially the steel guitar), strong backbeats and lyrics commonly about the working-class life.

Horrorcore.label: Horrorcore
Horrorcore.description: Lyrical and aesthetic focus on the macabre and morbid, with production generally of a darker nature than is typically found in other Hip Hop.

House.label: House
House.description: Large, hugely popular group of related Electronic Dance Music styles, initially developing from Disco in the early 1980s.

Huayno.label: Huayno
Huayno.description: Andean dance music that incorporates both pre-Hispanic and Western musical elements.

Hyphy.label: Hyphy
Hyphy.description: Party-focused style with upbeat tempos, heavy bass and prominent synths, all underpinned by a distinctive West Coast-style rhythm, claps on beats 2 and 4, and a strong kick drum on beats 1, 2, and either 3 or 4.

Ibiza Trance.label: Ibiza Trance
Ibiza Trance.description: Builds on the relaxing island atmosphere of Balearic Beat, though with a slightly faster tempo of around 130 BPM.

Indie Folk.label: Indie Folk
Indie Folk.description: Combines the melodic sensibilities and production style of Indie Rock and Indie Pop with acoustic instrumentation and influences from Folk, Singer-Songwriter, and sometimes Country.

Indie Pop.label: Indie Pop
Indie Pop.description: Back-to-basics Rock approach combining a less mainstream and DIY ethos with melodic, lighthearted and straightforward songwriting.

Indie Rock.label: Indie Rock
Indie Rock.description: Associated with a less mainstream-oriented and a DIY approach while often dabbling in Pop-informed melodic songwriting, eclecticism, and adopting an "authentic" or raw style.

Indie Surf.label: Indie Surf
Indie Surf.description: Reverb-drenched, nostalgic, and often lo-fi, with influences from Surf Rock and Vocal Surf; rose to popularity around 2009.

Indietronica.label: Indietronica
Indietronica.description: Leftfield or DIY approach characterised by simpler Pop-informed melodies, often incorporating vocals or live instrumentation alongside Electronic production.

Indigenous Andean Music.label: Indigenous Andean Music
Indigenous Andean Music.description: Diverse styles and genres developed by the indigenous peoples of the Andes.

Industrial Rock.label: Industrial Rock
Industrial Rock.description: Incorporates abrasive guitars and synthesizers inspired by Industrial and various Post-Industrial styles.

Italo Dance.label: Italo Dance
Italo Dance.description: Appeared in the 1990s after the decline of Italo-Disco, featuring rhythmic influence from that genre.

Italo House.label: Italo House
Italo House.description: Originated in Italy during the late 1980s, making use of anthemic pianos, diva vocals, and Dance-Pop song structures.

Italo Pop.label: Italo Pop
Italo Pop.description: Romantic style developed in Italy in the late 1950s including various influences such as Chanson and Schlager.

Italo-Disco.label: Italo-Disco
Italo-Disco.description: Produced primarily in Italy and is characterized by the use of synthesizers, drum machines, and sometimes vocoders to create a mechanical, Electronic version of Disco with futuristic atmosphere.

Jangle Pop.label: Jangle Pop
Jangle Pop.description: Treble-heavy guitars with arpeggiated melodies, distinct basslines, and often propulsive, repetitive strum-rhythms; frequently influenced by Post-Punk.

Jazz Pop.label: Jazz Pop
Jazz Pop.description: Pop that incorporates elements from Jazz.

Jerk Rap.label: Jerk Rap
Jerk Rap.description: Sparse beats, simple melodies, and party-centric lyrics geared towards a style of dance called “jerkin’.

Jersey Club.label: Jersey Club
Jersey Club.description: Emerged in Newark in the late 2000s; features fast tempos, hard kicks, and less prominent breakbeats.

Jook.label: Jook
Jook.description: Fast-paced; draws influence from Bounce, Miami Bass, Jersey Club and Dancehall.

Jug Band.label: Jug Band
Jug Band.description: Usually features a jug as the bass instrument as well as other homemade (or easily affordable) instruments, such as kazoos and washboards.

Jump-Up.label: Jump-Up
Jump-Up.description: Emerged in the mid-1990s, characterized by loud "wobbling" basslines, simpler drum loops, and a light-hearted and warm sound.

K-Pop.label: K-Pop
K-Pop.description: Contemporary style from South Korea, based on multiple mainstream influences such as Contemporary R&B, Dance-Pop, and Hip Hop.

Lambada.label: Lambada
Lambada.description: Agitated and sensual, with rhythms and sounds drawn from Carimbó, Cumbia and Forró, and dance moves inherited from Maxixe and Merengue.

Latin Disco.label: Latin Disco
Latin Disco.description: Disco that incorporates elements from Hispanic American Music styles.

Latin Jazz.label: Latin Jazz
Latin Jazz.description: Fusion with Latin American rhythms, especially those of African origin.

Latin Pop.label: Latin Pop
Latin Pop.description: Incorporates elements and tropes from Hispanic and Latin American forms of music into a commercial Pop context.

Latin Rap.label: Latin Rap
Latin Rap.description: Primarily done in Spanish; incorporates Hispanic American Music elements into its sound.

Latin Rock.label: Latin Rock
Latin Rock.description: Fusion of Rock, often Psychedelic Rock, with Latin American rhythms, especially Afro-Latin genres.

Light Music.label: Light Music
Light Music.description: Instrumental, typically through-composed style performed by an orchestra or small ensemble, with simpler arrangements and an emphasis on strong melodies.

Lounge.label: Lounge
Lounge.description: Emerged in the United States during the 1950s, emphasizing a chilled-out atmosphere with light instrumentation and sometimes vocals indebted to Vocal Jazz.

Mambo.label: Mambo
Mambo.description: Dance-oriented evolution from Danzón during the late 1930s.

Marching Band.label: Marching Band
Marching Band.description: Covering the music of Western-derived marching bands, primarily used for entertainment, competition, military, and parade settings, typically involving large-scale orchestras featuring a variety of instruments.

Mariachi.label: Mariachi
Mariachi.description: Mexican style performed in unique costumes by ensembles known as mariachi bands, with a distinctive sound combining high, bright tones with low and rich voicings.

Mashup.label: Mashup
Mashup.description: Songs created by mixing parts of two or more recordings together.

Mbaqanga.label: Mbaqanga
Mbaqanga.description: Emerged from the traditional Marabi Jazz and Swing as well as Kwela popular South African genres.

Medlodic Bass.label: Medlodic Bass
Medlodic Bass.description: Outgrowth of Melodic Dubstep with euphoric sound design taking influences from Future Bass, guitar melodies and pop-driven structures.

Melbourne Bounce.label: Melbourne Bounce
Melbourne Bounce.description: Offbeat basslines giving it a "bouncy" groove, and minimalistic, side-chained melodies often making use of Dutch House-influenced synths, horns or vocal chops, resulting in a flippant, party-oriented atmosphere.

Memphis Rap.label: Memphis Rap
Memphis Rap.description: Minimalistic half-time drum machine programming with droning bass, often paired with aggressive rapping and a double time flow.

Merseybeat.label: Merseybeat
Merseybeat.description: Light, highly melodic style of Beat popular in the UK during the early 1960s, named due to the abundance of bands from Liverpool beside the River Mersey.

Miami Bass.label: Miami Bass
Miami Bass.description: Sustained Roland TR-808 kick drums and faster tempos, along with a distinct Electro influence.

Microhouse.label: Microhouse
Microhouse.description: Stripped-down, minimal form of House, originating in the late 90s, which takes influence from Minimal Techno, Glitch and IDM.

Minimal Synth.label: Minimal Synth
Minimal Synth.description: Minimal and distinctly DIY take on Synthpop with a bleak and naive aesthetic.

Minneapolis Sound.label: Minneapolis Sound
Minneapolis Sound.description: Emerged in late 1970s Minneapolis, predominantly pioneered by Prince, characterized by glossy Contemporary R&B-influenced production techniques, angular New Wave-inspired rhythms, and a larger emphasis on electric guitar layers.

Mobb Music.label: Mobb Music
Mobb Music.description: San Francisco Bay Area production style building on slowed down Funk (often utilizing live elements).

Mod Revival.label: Mod Revival
Mod Revival.description: Late 1970s and early 1980s revival of Mod fashion and music, featuring influence from the nascent Punk Rock scene.

Moogsploitation.label: Moogsploitation
Moogsploitation.description: Moog synthesizer-led music often associated with a wave of Novelty records in the 1960s and 1970s.

Moombahton.label: Moombahton
Moombahton.description: Offshoot of Dutch House characterized by tempos in the 100-110 BPM range, Reggaetón-influenced rhythms, bass-heavy kicks and high-pitched synths.

Motown Sound.label: Motown Sound
Motown Sound.description: Succinct, catchy song structures; complex arrangements; Gospel-influenced vocals; highly melodic and rhythmic bass lines; 4-4 drum patterns; often syncopated piano parts; regular use of horn and string sections; and historically a distinctive reverb and treble-heavy production.

Music Hall.label: Music Hall
Music Hall.description: British theatrical entertainment popular in the 19th and 20th centuries, featuring light and comedic popular songs.

Musical Comedy.label: Musical Comedy
Musical Comedy.description: Heavily utilizes music in its delivery of humor.

Musical Parody.label: Musical Parody
Musical Parody.description: Practice of taking pre-existing music and restructuring or imitating its content lyrically, sonically, or by altering its intent, usually to create a humorous or ironic adaptation.

Mutant Disco.label: Mutant Disco
Mutant Disco.description: Danceable basslines, syncopated grooves, and angular guitar riffs, characterized by a spirit of experimentation and integration of disparate sounds.

Nashville Sound.label: Nashville Sound
Nashville Sound.description: Production style characterized by smooth vocals over a relaxed ensemble of guitars and rhythm section, and variously incorporating elements from other styles of Country and Pop.

Neo-Psychedelia.label: Neo-Psychedelia
Neo-Psychedelia.description: Developments in Psychedelia since the early 1980s, building on the drug-inspired styles developed in the 1960s.

Neo-Soul.label: Neo-Soul
Neo-Soul.description: Emerged during the mid-to-late 1990s; defined by a combination of Soul with the rhythmic percussion and groove of Hip Hop.

New Jack Swing.label: New Jack Swing
New Jack Swing.description: Mixes light Hip Hop & Funk production techniques with Contemporary R&B vocals and melodies as well as swinging and danceable drum grooves.

New Orleans R&B.label: New Orleans R&B
New Orleans R&B.description: Increased rhythmic complexity with added focus on piano and horns.

New Rave.label: New Rave
New Rave.description: High energy mid-2000s style combining the tight and punchy grooves of Dance-Punk Revival with synths and four-on-the-floor rhythms from genres like Electroclash and Electro House.

New Wave.label: New Wave
New Wave.description: Agitated and busy guitar melodies, jerky rhythms, often a heavy reliance on synthesizers, and typically intricate percussive sections, closely related to Post-Punk and influenced by Pop Rock and Electronic.

No Melody.label: No Melody
No Melody.description: Usage of minimalistic, repetitive midtempo drum patterns, and simple, prominent 808s.

Noise Pop.label: Noise Pop
Noise Pop.description: Arose in the mid-1980s; blend of poppier Rock songwriting and melodies with the dissonant, noisy timbres of distorted guitars and frequent use of guitar feedback.

Novelty.label: Novelty
Novelty.description: Intentionally becomes popular via the medium of humor, often described as 'silly'.

Nu Metal.label: Nu Metal
Nu Metal.description: Emphasizes bouncy, drop-tuned riffs, alternating vocal styles, and genre-bending, often incorporating Hip Hop and Funk Metal elements.

Nu-Disco.label: Nu-Disco
Nu-Disco.description: 21st-century trend mixing modern, polished Electronic production with the aesthetics, structures, and lively, organic beats of 1970s and 1980s Disco.

Old-Time.label: Old-Time
Old-Time.description: Pre-Bluegrass North American string band styles, particularly those rooted in Appalachian Folk Music.

Opera.label: Opera
Opera.description: European tradition which combines drama and distinctive vocal style in the context of Western Classical Music, usually performed as a theatrical work on stage.

Orchestral Music.label: Orchestral Music
Orchestral Music.description: Western Classical Music performed by an orchestra.

Outlaw Country.label: Outlaw Country
Outlaw Country.description: Rock-influenced reaction to mainstream Country Pop trends, featuring rebellious personas, darker lyrics, and Western-inspired outlaw imagery.

P-Funk.label: P-Funk
P-Funk.description: Features a "fat", monotonic drum sound, spaced-out synthesizers, "squelchy" basslines, tight, syncopated horns, and sometimes heavy guitars.

Paisley Underground.label: Paisley Underground
Paisley Underground.description: Emerged in Los Angeles in the early 1980s, featuring the energy of Power Pop and New Wave adorned with retro Psychedelia.

Pep Band.label: Pep Band
Pep Band.description: Performs at athletic events, and typically performs modified versions of Pop songs.

Philly Soul.label: Philly Soul
Philly Soul.description: Defined by lush arrangements, often borrowing upon the orchestral instrumentation seen on productions from the 1960s, with the commercial atmosphere exemplified by Pop Soul and the Motown Sound.

Phonk.label: Phonk
Phonk.description: Modernized, eclectic take on classic Memphis Rap production, featuring various influences from other Hip Hop genres, especially drum programming inspired by other Southern Hip Hop as well as frequent jazzy, funky elements somewhat akin to West Coast Hip Hop and from Electronic music.

Piano Rock.label: Piano Rock
Piano Rock.description: Primarily based around piano riffs and melodies, rather than having guitar as the focal instrument.

Pìobaireachd.label: Pìobaireachd
Pìobaireachd.description: Art tradition which originated in the Scottish Highlands, usually associated with the Great Highland bagpipes.

Pipe Band.label: Pipe Band
Pipe Band.description: Tradition originating in the Scottish military; standard instruments consist of 6-25 sets of Great Highland bagpipes, 3-10 military snare drums, 1-6 Scottish tenor drums, and one bass drum.

Political Hip Hop.label: Political Hip Hop
Political Hip Hop.description: Features explicitly political lyrics and themes.

Polka.label: Polka
Polka.description: Lively style and corresponding dance originating in Central and Eastern Europe, featuring a 2/4 cut time beat and instruments which vary by region.

Pop Punk.label: Pop Punk
Pop Punk.description: Blends the loud, fast-paced sound of Punk Rock with the melodic hooks of Pop.

Pop Rap.label: Pop Rap
Pop Rap.description: Fuses Hip Hop's rapping and production traits with Pop elements such as melodious vocals and light, catchy tunes.

Pop Reggae.label: Pop Reggae
Pop Reggae.description: Light, commercial form of Reggae designed to crossover and break through to a more mainstream Pop audience.

Pop Rock.label: Pop Rock
Pop Rock.description: Fuses conventional verse-chorus song structure and especially melodic songwriting with usage of guitars, drums, and propulsive rhythms.

Pop Soul.label: Pop Soul
Pop Soul.description: Fusion of Pop and Soul; originated in the 1960s as an attempt to give soul crossover success.

Post-Britpop.label: Post-Britpop
Post-Britpop.description: Emerged in the late 1990s as a Britpop expansion mainly characterized by acoustic-leaning instrumentation, lush string arrangements, and mid-tempo song structures, as well as less apparent lyrical concerns for Britain in favor of introspective, contemplative themes.

Post-Grunge.label: Post-Grunge
Post-Grunge.description: Streamlined derivative of Grunge mixing crunchy guitar riffs with more accessible, hook-based structures.

Post-Industrial.label: Post-Industrial
Post-Industrial.description: Umbrella group of genres that combine the dark, mechanical aesthetic of early Industrial music with more accessible genres, such as Rock and Electronic Dance Music.

Post-Punk.label: Post-Punk
Post-Punk.description: Emerged alongside the initial Punk Rock explosion in the mid-to-late 1970s, putting a greater emphasis on frequent experimentation, atmosphere, generally stripped-back instrumentation and, at times, angular-sounding guitars, throbbing bass lines, and interlocked drumming.

Post-Punk Revival.label: Post-Punk Revival
Post-Punk Revival.description: Incorporating the sounds and aesthetics of Post-Punk into Indie Rock, with jagged guitarwork, a dominant and danceable rhythm section, clean production, and poppy song structures.

Power Pop.label: Power Pop
Power Pop.description: Combines 1960s Pop melodies with loud power chords; characterized by prominent, sometimes jangly electric guitars, clear vocals, crisp harmonies, and economical arrangements.

Progressive House.label: Progressive House
Progressive House.description: Trance-influenced strain of House that grew to prominence in the early 1990s, using polished production and gradual progressions.

Progressive Pop.label: Progressive Pop
Progressive Pop.description: Milder counterpart to Progressive Rock, emerging in the late 1960s and introducing complex songwriting and arrangements into a Pop context.

Progressive Rock.label: Progressive Rock
Progressive Rock.description: Complex harmonies, instrumental virtuosity, and usually multi-section song structures featuring a broad range of instrumentation.

Progressive Soul.label: Progressive Soul
Progressive Soul.description: Greater harmonic complexity, less conventional song structures, and conceptual, socially conscious or introspective lyrical themes, incorporating diverse influences and instrumentation from Jazz, Funk, Rock, and beyond.

Proto-Punk.label: Proto-Punk
Proto-Punk.description: Used to describe what influenced or in some way resembled Punk Rock before that genre's commercial breakthrough in 1976.

Psychedelic Pop.label: Psychedelic Pop
Psychedelic Pop.description: Combines unconventional instrumentation with studio effects to create a psychedelic atmosphere within melodic Pop music.

Psychedelic Rock.label: Psychedelic Rock
Psychedelic Rock.description: Rock which emerged in the mid-1960s that often attempts to emulate or enhance the effects of psychedelic drugs.

Psychedelic Soul.label: Psychedelic Soul
Psychedelic Soul.description: Soul which incorporates the effects and instrumentation of Psychedelic Rock.

Psychobilly.label: Psychobilly
Psychobilly.description: Cross-pollination of Punk Rock and Rockabilly.

Pub Rock.label: Pub Rock
Pub Rock.description: Stripped-down style developed in the United Kingdom in the early to mid-1970s, mainly as a reaction to the perceived excess and pretension of Progressive Rock and Glam Rock.

Punk Rock.label: Punk Rock
Punk Rock.description: Fast tempos, loud and distorted riffs, simple songs, frequent use of power chords, and shouted vocals, originating in the mid-1970s in response to commercial, sentimental, and complex Rock.

Ragga.label: Ragga
Ragga.description: Hardcore style with a deejay (equivalent to an MC in Hip Hop) rapping over predominantly Electronic rhythms.

Rap Metal.label: Rap Metal
Rap Metal.description: Features rapped vocals, typically formed around heavy, mid-tempo, and groove-oriented playing in addition to elements of Hip Hop beats such as record scratching.

Rap Rock.label: Rap Rock
Rap Rock.description: Fusion of Hip Hop and Rock, often featuring the former’s rhythmic vocal delivery and the latter’s heavy, distorted instrumentation.

Ratchet.label: Ratchet
Ratchet.description: Club-oriented style defined by a repeated synth melody and a sparse drum pattern often accompanied by a "hey" chant.

Reggae Rock.label: Reggae Rock
Reggae Rock.description: Incorporates the offbeat rhythms and staccato "skank" guitar of Reggae.

Reggaetón.label: Reggaetón
Reggaetón.description: Rooted in Dancehall and mainly characterized by its heavy emphasis on the "dembow" rhythm; developed in Puerto Rico in the early 1990s under heavy influence from Hip Hop.

Rhythm & Blues.label: Rhythm & Blues
Rhythm & Blues.description: Predominantly African American style that retains the defined backbeats, Blues chord progressions/melodies and passionate singing/shouting of Jump Blues, while appealing more to an urban audience with smaller bands and reduced Jazz influence.

Rock & Roll.label: Rock & Roll
Rock & Roll.description: Developed in the United States and popularized in the 1950s, featuring prominent Blues scales, poppy structures, and strong rhythms.

Rock Andino.label: Rock Andino
Rock Andino.description: Emerged in the Andean countries in the late 1960s and early 1970s, incorporating traditional instruments and harmonies from Indigenous Andean Music within a Rock format.

Rock Opera.label: Rock Opera
Rock Opera.description: Hybrid of rock and, sometimes, musical theatre stylings with heavily rock-tinged songs combining to tell a coherent story.

Rockabilly.label: Rockabilly
Rockabilly.description: Blends the sound of Country with that of Rhythm & Blues, often accompanied by driving guitars, frantic vocals, and slap-back echo in the bass.

Rocksteady.label: Rocksteady
Rocksteady.description: Jamaican popular music that blossomed briefly between 1966 and 1968 as an evolution of Ska, being slower, more relaxed, and soulful.

Romanian Popcorn.label: Romanian Popcorn
Romanian Popcorn.description: Emerged in the second half of the 2000s in Romania, often featuring brass melodies and "staccato" pluck grooves.

Roots Rock.label: Roots Rock
Roots Rock.description: Consciously and predominantly incorporates elements of its predecessors and original roots such as Country, Blues, Rhythm & Blues, Rock & Roll, and American Folk Music, but also many other American traditions.

Rumba Flamenca.label: Rumba Flamenca
Rumba Flamenca.description: Flamenco subgenre, derived from the exchange of influences between Spain and Latin America.

Russian Romance.label: Russian Romance
Russian Romance.description: Popular Russian genre of melancholy balladry.

Samba.label: Samba
Samba.description: Originated in the 19th century from a blend of various rhythmic styles of African Music, forming a new sound, structure, and a dance of the same name.

Satire.label: Satire
Satire.description: Human or individual vices, follies, or shortcomings are ridiculed in an ironic manner.

Schlager.label: Schlager
Schlager.description: Light, sentimental, melodic pop music common mainly in northern and central Europe.

Scottish Folk Music.label: Scottish Folk Music
Scottish Folk Music.description: Traditional Folk Music of the Scottish people.

Show Tunes.label: Show Tunes
Show Tunes.description: Originally composed to forward the story or enhance the characters or other plot elements of Broadway productions and other stage music.

Singer-Songwriter.label: Singer-Songwriter
Singer-Songwriter.description: Heavily focused on lyrics and songwriting, with musical accompaniment tending to take lower precedence.

Ska.label: Ska
Ska.description: Originated in Jamaica in the late 1950s, characterized by a signature off-beat rhythm (referred to as the ‘skank’ rhythm), chop-style guitar work, busy bass lines and a use of horn sections.

Sketch Comedy.label: Sketch Comedy
Sketch Comedy.description: Performers assume a dramatic setting (rather than addressing the audience directly), often as fictional characters.

Skiffle.label: Skiffle
Skiffle.description: Grew out of British Trad Jazz, drawing influence from 1920s Jug Bands and the strong rhythms of 1950s Honky Tonk and Rock & Roll.

Slap House.label: Slap House
Slap House.description: Taking influences from Brazilian Bass and Future House, characterized by prominent, "bouncy" bassline and simple, upbeat melodies.

Smooth Jazz.label: Smooth Jazz
Smooth Jazz.description: Simple melodies and slick production influenced by Pop and R&B.

Smooth Soul.label: Smooth Soul
Smooth Soul.description: Slow tempo ballads with silky, often sensual, vocals and light, polished arrangements, creating an intimate, romantic atmosphere.

Soft Rock.label: Soft Rock
Soft Rock.description: Clean production and light instrumentation paired with harmonious, radio-friendly songwriting; gained huge commercial success in the 1970s.

Son jarocho.label: Son jarocho
Son jarocho.description: Mexican Folk Music from the Gulf Coast Mexican state of Veracruz, with both Spanish and indigenous influences.

Sophisti-Pop.label: Sophisti-Pop
Sophisti-Pop.description: Incorporates influences from Jazz, classic R&B and Soul along with a slick, polished production style while retaining elements from its New Wave roots.

Soul Blues.label: Soul Blues
Soul Blues.description: Shares similarities with Soul and incorporates the eponymous soulful inflections of Gospel, Jump Blues and soul into the vocals.

Sound Collage.label: Sound Collage
Sound Collage.description: Compositional method emphasizing the rough layering of sampled elements, presenting an impression similar to a visual collage.

Southern Metal.label: Southern Metal
Southern Metal.description: Blues-influenced style that primarily incorporates Sludge Metal and Stoner Metal with metallic Southern Rock riffs.

Southern Rock.label: Southern Rock
Southern Rock.description: Emerged from the American South in the early 1970s, blending Blues Rock, Hard Rock, and Rock & Roll with elements of Country and Contemporary Folk.

Southern Soul.label: Southern Soul
Southern Soul.description: Soul primarily from the American South that was popular in the 1960s and 1970s, with a gritty and raw sound that places an emphasis on the groove, making it highly danceable.

Space Age Pop.label: Space Age Pop
Space Age Pop.description: Inspired by the excitement and optimism of the Space Age during the 1950s and 1960s, frequently experimenting with futuristic sounds made possible by contemporary technology.

Space Disco.label: Space Disco
Space Disco.description: Emerged in Europe in the mid-to-late 1970s, characterized by layers of oscillating synth, space-oriented sound effects, and a futuristic sound inspired by contemporary sci-fi media.

Spaghetti Western.label: Spaghetti Western
Spaghetti Western.description: Epic, cinematic sound evoking imagery of the Wild West, featuring a Surf Rock-inspired guitar tone while taking inspiration from the melodies and harmonies of traditional Mexican Music.

Standards.label: Standards
Standards.description: Traditional compositions originating from the 1910s and onward which have become widely known, performed, and recorded across the world.

Steel Band.label: Steel Band
Steel Band.description: Ensembles made out of steel drums that often perform Standards, Calypso, and Western Classical Music with densely rhythmic and colorful arrangements.

Stomp and Holler.label: Stomp and Holler
Stomp and Holler.description: Highlights driving "stomping" rhythms and shouted group vocal harmonies, often drawing influences from Pop Rock, Indie Folk, and other genres, while maintaining a characteristically mellow and commercial sound.

Stoner Rock.label: Stoner Rock
Stoner Rock.description: Psychedelic timbres, raw production, and stylistic influence from Doom Metal and Heavy Psych.

Sunshine Pop.label: Sunshine Pop
Sunshine Pop.description: Emerged in the mid-1960s, characterized by a cheerful or wistful sound, light, lush production, and prominent vocal harmonies.

Surf Rock.label: Surf Rock
Surf Rock.description: Short instrumental songs with Rock & Roll rhythms overlaid with cheerful melodies played on reverb-drenched tremolo guitar.

Swamp Rock.label: Swamp Rock
Swamp Rock.description: Incorporates influences from the musical styles and traditions of Louisiana.

Swing.label: Swing
Swing.description: Distinctive rhythm and large backing rhythm section over which solos are performed.

Swing Revival.label: Swing Revival
Swing Revival.description: Born out of 1990s alternative music; sometimes incorporating elements from newer genres such as Rockabilly, Ska, and Punk Rock.

Synth Funk.label: Synth Funk
Synth Funk.description: Stripped-down and futuristic, driven by synthesizer melodies and often incorporating drum machines.

Synth Punk.label: Synth Punk
Synth Punk.description: Appropriates the harsh elements of Punk Rock but replaces the predominance of guitars with synthesizers and drum machines.

Synthpop.label: Synthpop
Synthpop.description: Led by melodic synthesizer sounds, often with reverberating drum machine patterns; commonly associated with the 1980s.

Synthwave.label: Synthwave
Synthwave.description: Rooted in 1980s synth music, characterized by large, throbbing "retro" synths and visual elements, often features upbeat percussion and/or epic, cinematic atmospheres.

Tape Music.label: Tape Music
Tape Music.description: Explores the nature of audio recorded to magnetic tape and the act of playing sound from it.

Tech House.label: Tech House
Tech House.description: Emerged in the mid-1990s taking influences from Techno, broadly characterized by minimalistic and repetitive melodies, focus on the bassline, and constant groove recognizable by prominent off-beat hi-hats.

Techno Bass.label: Techno Bass
Techno Bass.description: Minimal, stripped-down with an emphasis on low-frequency bass partitions.

Teen Pop.label: Teen Pop
Teen Pop.description: Oriented to teen audiences and almost always sung by teens.

Tejano Music.label: Tejano Music
Tejano Music.description: Mexican-American style infusing the conjunto Norteño ensemble with European dances and American popular music.

Television Music.label: Television Music
Television Music.description: Soundtrack or score that accompanies a televised program.

Tex-Mex.label: Tex-Mex
Tex-Mex.description: Mixes Rock & Roll, Blues, Country, and several types of Hispanic American Music, particularly Tejano Music.

Third Wave Ska.label: Third Wave Ska
Third Wave Ska.description: Expanded on the 2 Tone period, adding further Rock elements resulting in a heavier sound.

Toytown Pop.label: Toytown Pop
Toytown Pop.description: Niche offshoot of British Psychedelic Pop most popular in the late 1960s and early 70s; typically featuring strong influence from Music Hall and Novelty, as well as the culture of Victorian and Edwardian Britain, and characterised by a whimsical, childlike atmosphere.

Traditional Country.label: Traditional Country
Traditional Country.description: Closely aligned with American Folk Music and Blues roots, from country's inception in Appalachia and the American South until the popularity of Honky Tonk and Country Pop in the 1950s.

Traditional Pop.label: Traditional Pop
Traditional Pop.description: Predominant form of Western commercial popular music in the pre-Rock & Roll era, particularly in the USA and starting with Tin Pan Alley; typically vocal-oriented with an orchestral arrangement.

Trap.label: Trap
Trap.description: Originated in Atlanta in the early 2000s; distinctive fast hi-hat sound and heavy bass at moderate tempos.

Trap [EDM].label: Trap [EDM]
Trap [EDM].description: Trap-influenced syncopated percussion with 808 samples and fast-paced hi-hats.

Trap Soul.label: Trap Soul
Trap Soul.description: Mixes passionate vocals derived from Contemporary R&B with laid-back, nocturnal and atmospheric Trap production.

Trip Hop.label: Trip Hop
Trip Hop.description: Evokes a surreal, trippy, dreamy, and sometimes dark atmosphere with offbeat turntable scratches, light vocal melodies, and Hip Hop-influenced beats.

Tropical House.label: Tropical House
Tropical House.description: Offshoot of Deep House featuring slow tempos around 100-115 BPM, light percussion and acoustic instrumentation, setting out to create a relaxed, summery, beach-like atmosphere.

Tropical Rock.label: Tropical Rock
Tropical Rock.description: Combines influences from Country Rock and Caribbean Music to create an acoustic and breezy "island" themed sound.

Tropipop.label: Tropipop
Tropipop.description: Subgenre of Colombian origin fusing Vallenato instrumentation into a Latin Pop context.

Truck Driving Country.label: Truck Driving Country
Truck Driving Country.description: Concerns the lifestyle of professional truck drivers, often drawing from Country Rock and Bakersfield Sound musically.

Turkish Pop.label: Turkish Pop
Turkish Pop.description: Started in the late 1950s, when Turkish artists started to write their own lyrics to Western popular tunes.

Turntablism.label: Turntablism
Turntablism.description: Manipulating and re-appropriating vinyl records using a turntable and DJ mixer in a Hip Hop context, often featuring constant scratching and beat juggling between two or more recordings into original, distinctive tracks.

Twerk.label: Twerk
Twerk.description: Fast-paced; draws influence from Bounce and Miami Bass.

Twist.label: Twist
Twist.description: Simple, fast, and energetic dance popular in the early 1960s.

UK Hard House.label: UK Hard House
UK Hard House.description: Defined by offbeat bass patterns, compressed 909-style drums, buildup-drop structures, hoover sounds, and tempos between 120 and 155 BPM.

UK Street Soul.label: UK Street Soul
UK Street Soul.description: Emerged in the late 1980s and early 1990s; combines raw basslines and breakbeats with Contemporary R&B/Soul vocals.

Urban Contemporary Gospel.label: Urban Contemporary Gospel
Urban Contemporary Gospel.description: Inspired by Gospel music, featuring Christian lyrics adapted to contemporary rhythms and styles.

Urban Cowboy.label: Urban Cowboy
Urban Cowboy.description: Melds Country and 1970s L.A. soft Pop, sometimes leaning towards Disco.

Vaudeville.label: Vaudeville
Vaudeville.description: Performed in American musical theaters typically as part of a lowbrow, often comedic variety performance.

Vocal Group.label: Vocal Group
Vocal Group.description: Ensemble of multiple vocalists who generally sing popular music, generally with light backing or unaccompanied.

Vocal Jazz.label: Vocal Jazz
Vocal Jazz.description: Focused on vocals, generally using a more traditional, poppy strong structure and reducing the role of improvisation.

Vocal Surf.label: Vocal Surf
Vocal Surf.description: Appropriates the sounds of instrumental Surf Rock, combining surfing- and car-themed lyrics with a style that draws primarily from Doo-Wop and Rock & Roll.

Vocal Trance.label: Vocal Trance
Vocal Trance.description: Melodic, polished, Pop-like, and vocal-focused with around 120-145 BPM and frequent overlap with Progressive Trance, Uplifting Trance, and Euro Trance.

Waltz.label: Waltz
Waltz.description: Typically in a 3/4 time signature and popular in the 18th century in central Europe, as it accompanied the ballroom dance of the same name.

Western Swing.label: Western Swing
Western Swing.description: Blend of Traditional Country string instrumentation with the driving rhythms of Jazz and regional dance traditions, often prominently featuring fiddle and steel guitar.

Wonky.label: Wonky
Wonky.description: Off-kilter fusion of Dubstep and Hip Hop which first developed in the mid-to-late 2000s.

Worldbeat.label: Worldbeat
Worldbeat.description: Pairs elements from diverse sources from across the globe with contemporary, uplifting Westernised production, created primarily for a Western audience.

Xian Psych.label: Xian Psych
Xian Psych.description: Portrays Christian and biblical themes through a surreal, transcendent atmosphere, occasionally drawing influence from Christian Liturgical Music.

Yacht Rock.label: Yacht Rock
Yacht Rock.description: Slick Pop Rock influenced by smoother R&B styles, popular in the late 1970s and often associated with California.

Yodeling.label: Yodeling
Yodeling.description: Vocal technique that arose in many cultures around the world as a form of calling and communication.

Zolo.label: Zolo
Zolo.description: Retrospective term grouping artists that fuse elements of New Wave and Progressive Rock with a flamboyant, quirky aesthetic and often the energy of Punk.

Zydeco.label: Zydeco
Zydeco.description: Uptempo accordion-led dance style mixing Creole and Cajun folk with African American popular music influences.

Fife and Drum Corps.label: Fife and Drum Corps
Fife and Drum Corps.description: Derived from military field music, primarily uses fifes and drums.

Volkstümliche Musik.label: Volkstümliche Musik
Volkstümliche Musik.description: Light Pop with borrowings from German Folk Music (volksmusik), often misleadingly labeled as being volksmusik.

Cinematic Classical.label: Cinematic Classical
Cinematic Classical.description: Orchestral compositions generally associated with soundtracks to modern high-budget films, games, and other non-live media.

Electric Blues.label: Electric Blues
Electric Blues.description: Major development in sound and reach of Blues when it became possible to amplify the instruments of small combos.

Hapa haole.label: Hapa haole
Hapa haole.description: Hawaiian musical traditions adapted to popular American Jazz and Pop styles.

Mod.label: Mod
Mod.description: 1960s youth subculture in the United Kingdom, drawing from British Rhythm & Blues, Beat, Garage Rock, and Pop sensibilities alongside a distinctive fashion style.

Sound Effects.label: Sound Effects
Sound Effects.description: Non-musical audio works, including recorded sounds and sounds recreated synthetically or by other means, which are either sounds of known objects or abstract noise manifestations.

Sea Shanty.label: Sea Shanty
Sea Shanty.description: Songs originally sung by sailors to coordinate work on ships.

British Brass Band.label: British Brass Band
British Brass Band.description: An ensemble consisting of a standardized range of brass and percussion instruments whose performances are based on the British tradition established in the early 19th century.

Chamber Music.label: Chamber Music
Chamber Music.description: Western Classical Music performed by a small ensemble, usually only a few performers.

String Quartet.label: String Quartet
String Quartet.description: Small scale composition for an ensemble that consists of two violins, one viola, and one cello, with complex interplay and multiple (usually four) movements.

Raga Rock.label: Raga Rock
Raga Rock.description: Influenced by South Asian Music, featuring a droning compositional quality and commonly incorporating instruments like sitar, tambura, and tablas.

Experimental Rock.label: Experimental Rock
Experimental Rock.description: Eschews accessibility and convention, experimenting with song structures, time signatures, rhythm, dissonance, instrumentation, noise, electronics, studio manipulation, and other techniques not traditionally associated with Rock.

English Underground.label: English Underground
English Underground.description: Countercultural scene in the United Kingdom, centred around London clubs like UFO and Middle Earth, which peaked in 1966 and 1967.

Garage Psych.label: Garage Psych
Garage Psych.description: Emerged from mid-1960s US Garage Rock; characterised by fuzz guitars, cheap organs, experimentation with psychedelic effects and surreal lyricism, and a retained emphasis on raw energy and the pop song format.

Noise Rock.label: Noise Rock
Noise Rock.description: Explores dissonance and distortion through unconventional playing and guitar effects, commonly augmented by lo-fi production and bursts of feedback.

Acid Rock.label: Acid Rock
Acid Rock.description: Heavier with an emphasis on instrumental jamming and "wah" guitar effects.

Radio Broadcast Recordings.label: Radio Broadcast Recordings
Radio Broadcast Recordings.description: Recordings of transmissions sent by radio waves, used for sending communications to a wide audience.

Jamaican Ska.label: Jamaican Ska
Jamaican Ska.description: Originated in Jamaica with groups who combined Jazz and Rhythm & Blues with rhythms of traditional Caribbean Music like Mento and Calypso.

Soul.label: Soul
Soul.description: Originated in the African American culture of the 20th century, resulting from the combination of the ecstatic vocals of Gospel with the upbeat instrumentation of Rhythm & Blues, consisting of guitar, bass and percussion.

Chanson.label: Chanson
Chanson.description: French-language Singer-Songwriters for whom the quality of the text is essential and where the rhythm follows that of the text.

Ragtime.label: Ragtime
Ragtime.description: Emerged in the 1890s; piano-led and characterized by a heavily syncopated rhythm.

CCM.label: CCM
CCM.description: Contemporary Christian Music; Popular music focused on Christian spirituality and faith.

Aussie Pub Rock.label: Aussie Pub Rock
Aussie Pub Rock.description: Straightforward Rock acts associated with the Australian pub circuit of the 1970s and 1980s.

Vedic Chant.label: Vedic Chant
Vedic Chant.description: Sacred Hindu chants rooted in the ancient Vedas, and primarily recited as mantras, requiring strict precision and diligence.

Laurel Canyon Scene.label: Laurel Canyon Scene
Laurel Canyon Scene.description: 1960s and 1970s Rock counterculture originating from the Laurel Canyon neighborhood of Los Angeles, highly influential to the development of contemporary Folk Rock and Soft Rock.

Chiptune.label: Chiptune
Chiptune.description: Used primarily in the 1980s within the context of 8-bit Video Game Music, combining simple synthesized waveforms and noise in a compositionally limited but overtly melodic style.

Progressive Electronic.label: Progressive Electronic
Progressive Electronic.description: Synthesizer-driven genre focusing on developing, often longform compositions and drawing inspiration from various sources such as Progressive Rock, Western Classical Music, and Ambient.

Operetta.label: Operetta
Operetta.description: Light opera interspersed with spoken dialogues, being usually satirical and/or humorous in nature and centered around romantic plots and dancing scenes.

CBGB Scene.label: CBGB Scene
CBGB Scene.description: Early Punk scene built around artists who frequently played at the East Village's CBGB club in the mid-1970s.

New Pop.label: New Pop
New Pop.description: British-centric movement of 1980s acts utilising Post-Punk ideals in a mainstream Pop context, characterised by cross-style experimentation and a strong focus on visual aesthetics.

New Romantic.label: New Romantic
New Romantic.description: UK cultural movement associated with the early 1980s and defined by a distinct, flamboyant fashion sense.

Akron Sound.label: Akron Sound
Akron Sound.description: Early Punk and New Wave scene in Akron, Ohio, and nearby Kent from the mid-1970s to the early 1980s.

Neo-Prog.label: Neo-Prog
Neo-Prog.description: Emerged in the early 1980s in the United Kingdom from the influence of Symphonic Prog, utilizing a synth-driven sound that focuses on Pop-informed melodicism while de-emphasizing guitar riffing.

West Coast Hip Hop.label: West Coast Hip Hop
West Coast Hip Hop.description: Artists who originate from the West Coast region of the United States.

Sunset Strip Glam Metal.label: Sunset Strip Glam Metal
Sunset Strip Glam Metal.description: 1980s Glam Metal scene based in West Hollywood, California, United States.

Choral.label: Choral
Choral.description: Choir-sung classical works covering periods from medieval music (an early form being Gregorian Chant) up to present day.

East Coast Hip Hop.label: East Coast Hip Hop
East Coast Hip Hop.description: Highly influential Hip Hop scene that developed in New York City, particularly in the South Bronx, during the 1970s.

Drinking Song.label: Drinking Song
Drinking Song.description: Songs meant to be sung locally while consuming alcohol, often in bars, pubs, or taverns.

Salsa.label: Salsa
Salsa.description: Often incorporating congas, timbales and bongos as well as smaller instruments, such as maracas, clave and güiro.

Bomba.label: Bomba
Bomba.description: Percussive style from Puerto Rico, generally considered to be derived from West Africa.

Midwest Hip Hop.label: Midwest Hip Hop
Midwest Hip Hop.description: Hip Hop from artists based within the midwestern region of the United States.

Southern Hip Hop.label: Southern Hip Hop
Southern Hip Hop.description: Hip Hop from artists based in the Southern region of the United States.

Bay Area Hip Hop.label: Bay Area Hip Hop
Bay Area Hip Hop.description: Hip Hop from the San Francisco Bay Area, known for its bass-heavy production, Funk-inspired instrumentation, and a unique regional vernacular.

Neon Pop Punk.label: Neon Pop Punk
Neon Pop Punk.description: Emerged from Scene culture; combines the upbeat tempos and catchy melodies of Pop Punk and Power Pop with synthesizers, auto-tuned vocals, or Dance beats, emphasizing a distinctly polished, hook-oriented sound.

Bloghouse.label: Bloghouse
Bloghouse.description: Internet-based movement from the mid-to-late-2000s which broadly distilled scenes most associated with online music blogging, mp3-sharing and remix culture, intersecting within Dance styles such as Electroclash, Electro House, New Rave and Nu-Disco in a brash and carefree fashion.

Post-Dubstep.label: Post-Dubstep
Post-Dubstep.description: Minimalistic, atmospheric sound design and eclectic drum patterns, derived from but not fitting Dubstep.

UK Hip Hop.label: UK Hip Hop
UK Hip Hop.description: Scene of the United Kingdom, influenced by American rap.

Hypnagogic Pop.label: Hypnagogic Pop
Hypnagogic Pop.description: 21st century style that refracts the pop culture of past decades through surreal, low-fidelity production and emulation of outdated media formats or equipment.

red dirt.label: Red Dirt
red dirt.description: Oklahoman and Texan acts descended from the Stillwater, Oklahoma independent Country scene of the 1980s.

Bassline.label: Bassline
Bassline.description: Strong emphasis on intricate basslines as well as four-on-the-floor beat patterns.
